description: VLM perception returns imprecise bounding boxes. Use segmentation mask contours to compute tight refined bboxes, return them in the segment API, and have the frontend replace VLM bboxes with refined ones so all downstream steps (SVG overlay, inpaint, planner) use accurate spatial data.

userStories.title: Compute tight bbox from contour polygon
userStories.description: As the system, I need to compute a tight axis-aligned bounding box from the mask contour so downstream consumers can use accurate spatial data instead of the VLM's rough estimate.
acceptanceCriteria: New function compute_tight_bbox(contour: list[list[float]]) -> list[float] | None in backend/app/services/contour.py
Returns [x, y, width, height] computed from min/max of contour vertex coordinates
Returns None when contour is empty or has fewer than 3 points
Unit test: square contour [[10,10],[30,10],[30,30],[10,30]] produces bbox [10, 10, 20, 20]
Unit test: empty contour returns None
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Pure utility function — add it to the existing contour.py service module next to extract_contour and compute_centroid. Keep the function simple: just iterate contour points for min/max x/y. Add tests in tests/test_contour.py.

userStories.title: Return refined_bbox in segment API response
userStories.description: As the system, I need the segment API response to include a refined bounding box per layer so the frontend can replace the VLM's rough estimate with a precise one.
acceptanceCriteria: Layer model in backend/app/routers/segment.py gains field: refined_bbox: list[float] | None = None
In the segment route's main loop, after extracting contour, call compute_tight_bbox(contour_pts) and assign to layer.refined_bbox
When contour is empty (no foreground mask), refined_bbox stays None
Frontend api.ts Layer interface gains: refined_bbox?: [number, number, number, number] | null
Existing segment tests updated to assert refined_bbox is present in the response when a mask exists
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: This story wires the compute_tight_bbox function from US-026 into the segment route. The Layer Pydantic model is in segment.py around line 51. Import compute_tight_bbox from app.services.contour. The frontend Layer interface is in frontend/src/lib/api.ts.

userStories.title: Frontend replaces VLM bboxes with refined bboxes after segmentation
userStories.description: As the frontend, I need to update element bboxes with refined values from the segment response so that SVG overlays, inpaint masks, and animation planning all use accurate coordinates.
acceptanceCriteria: In Editor.vue runSegment(), after receiving the segment response, iterate layers and update elements.value: for each layer with non-null refined_bbox, find the matching element by id and overwrite its bbox
SVG rect overlays automatically display the updated bboxes (they bind to el.bbox reactively)
The runInpaint() call that follows uses the updated bboxes (no extra changes needed — it reads from elements.value)
The planAnimation() call uses updated bboxes (no extra changes needed — it reads from elements.value)
Elements without a matching layer or with null refined_bbox keep their original VLM bbox
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is a frontend-only change in Editor.vue. The key is to add a few lines in runSegment() after `layers.value = response.layers` that loop through layers and update element bboxes. Since elements is a Vue ref, mutating the bbox property will trigger reactive updates in the SVG overlay template.

userStories.title: Improve VLM perception prompt bbox tightness guidance
userStories.description: As the system, I need the perception prompt to guide the VLM toward producing tighter bounding boxes that closely wrap the visible pixels of each element.
acceptanceCriteria: PERCEPTION_PROMPT in backend/app/routers/perception.py updated with explicit bbox tightness instructions
Prompt states: the bbox must be the smallest axis-aligned rectangle that contains ALL visible pixels of the element, with no more than a few pixels of padding
Prompt states: do NOT let the bbox be significantly larger than the element — avoid excess whitespace inside the box
Prompt states: do NOT let any part of the element fall outside the bbox — the box must fully enclose the element
Existing perception tests pass unchanged
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: This is a prompt-only change — modify only the PERCEPTION_PROMPT string in perception.py. Add the bbox guidance BEFORE the sub-part decomposition section. Keep it concise (3-4 sentences). This reduces the initial bbox error so the segment refinement has less work to do.

userStories.title: GrabCut two-pass refinement using tight bbox
userStories.description: As the system, I need the GrabCut fallback to use a two-pass approach — first with the rough VLM bbox, then with a tight bbox derived from the first pass's contour — for better segmentation accuracy.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/app/config.py gains function grabcut_two_pass() -> bool reading env var GRABCUT_TWO_PASS (default true)
_grabcut_segment() in segment.py implements two-pass logic: pass 1 with VLM bbox → extract contour → compute tight bbox → pass 2 with tight bbox
If pass 1 mask has foreground area < 5% of bbox area, skip pass 2 and fall back to rectangular mask (GrabCut failure case)
Two-pass can be disabled via GRABCUT_TWO_PASS=false for performance-sensitive environments
Existing fallback tests still pass (the test image is solid color, GrabCut may fail on it — this is fine, the rectangular fallback catches it)
Total fallback processing time per element ≤ 8s on a medium-resolution image
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Modify _grabcut_segment() in segment.py. The two-pass logic wraps the existing single-pass code: run it once, check mask foreground area, if sufficient extract contour → compute tight bbox → run GrabCut again with the tighter rect. The second pass reuses the same scaled working image. Add the config function to config.py following the existing pattern.
